entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures, including sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: Region in Africa characterized by poverty, food insecurity, and specific labor needs

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: Region in Asia characterized by poverty and specific labor needs

entities.name: INCOME GROWTH
entities.description: The increase in the amount of money that individuals or households earn

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: POVERTY ALLEVIATION
entities.description: The process of reducing the number of people living in poverty

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The maintenance of ecological balance and resource availability for future generations

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: The state of being extremely poor

entities.name: FOOD INSECURITY
entities.description: The lack of consistent access to enough food for an active, healthy life

entities.name: CHILD MALNUTRITION
entities.description: Deficiencies, excesses, or imbalances in a child's intake of energy and/or nutrients

entities.name: GENDER INEQUALITIES
entities.description: Unequal treatment or perceptions of individuals based on their gender

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: Grass crops cultivated for their edible grains, serving as staple foods in the SAT

entities.name: LIVESTOCK PRODUCTS
entities.description: Goods derived from livestock, such as meat, milk, and eggs

entities.name: FEEDGRAINS
entities.description: Grains used primarily for animal feed, including sorghum and millet

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A drought-tolerant cereal crop used for food and feed

entities.name: MILLET
entities.description: A group of small-seeded grasses cultivated as cereal crops

entities.name: WATER
entities.description: A critical resource and primary constraint in the semi-arid tropics

entities.name: WATER-USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of a plant to produce biomass per unit of water used

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand periods of water shortage

entities.name: GENES
entities.description: Segments of DNA that can be identified to improve crop traits

entities.name: CROP AND SYSTEMS MODELING
entities.description: The use of mathematical models to simulate crop growth and agricultural systems

entities.name: WATERSHED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of organizing and guiding land and water resource use within a watershed

entities.name: WATER POLICY
entities.description: Guidelines and regulations governing the use and management of water resources

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients to plant growth

entities.name: INTEGRATED SOIL WATER AND NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT APPROACH
entities.description: A holistic strategy to manage soil, water, and nutrients for improved productivity

entities.name: NUTRIENT-USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of a plant to utilize nutrients effectively for growth and yield

entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in the quality of land due to human activities or natural processes

entities.name: LABOR-INTENSIVE TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Agricultural practices that require a high amount of human labor

entities.name: LABOR-SAVING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Agricultural practices designed to reduce the amount of human labor required

entities.name: HIV/AIDS
entities.description: A health crisis that acts as a constraint to labor availability in certain regions

entities.name: SOUTHERN AND EASTERN AFRICA
entities.description: Specific regions in Africa heavily impacted by HIV/AIDS

entities.name: POVERTY-ENVIRONMENTAL DEGRADATION TREADMILL
entities.description: A cycle where poverty leads to environmental degradation, which in turn exacerbates poverty

entities.name: MARKETING AND TRANSACTION COSTS
entities.description: Costs associated with the sale and exchange of agricultural products

entities.name: INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: The use of computers and telecommunications to strengthen research and farming capacities

entities.name: GENDER-SENSITIVE TECHNOLOGY OPTIONS
entities.description: Agricultural technologies designed to address the specific needs and constraints of different genders
